title: Basananthe longifolia
intro: Basananthe longifolia, also known as African spurge, is a plant species belonging to the family Passifloraceae. It has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Basananthe longifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Basananthe longifolia is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of traditional remedies such as ointments and salves.
taxonomy: Basananthe longifolia is classified within the family Passifloraceae, commonly known as the passionflower family. This family comprises over 1,000 species of flowering plants. Basananthe longifolia is a species of flowering plant in the genus Basananthe. The genus Basananthe is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa.
